name: Chinese Radicals
description: The 54 building blocks that make Chinese characters stop looking like random strokes. No Chinese required: see a component, name what it means. Once you know 氵 is water and 讠 is speech, thousands of characters become guessable. The perfect on-ramp to Essential Chinese.

fields.front: What does the radical 亻 mean?
fields.extra: The squeezed side-form of 人 (rén, person). Spot it in 你 (you), 他 (he), 们 (the plural marker): words about people.

fields.front: What does the radical 口 mean?
fields.extra: kǒu; a literal open mouth. In 吃 (eat), 喝 (drink), 叫 (call), and the spoken particles 吗, 呢, 啊: anything said or eaten.

fields.front: What does the radical 扌 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 手 (shǒu, hand). In 打 (hit), 拉 (pull), 抱 (hug): actions done with the hands.

fields.front: What does the radical 心 (sitting at the BOTTOM of a character) mean?
fields.extra: xīn. Thoughts and feelings rest on a heart: 想 (think), 忘 (forget), 感 (feel).

fields.front: What does the radical 忄 (a vertical line with two dots, on the LEFT) mean?
fields.extra: The heart 心 squeezed thin to fit the left side. Emotions again: 忙 (busy), 怕 (afraid), 慢 (slow).

fields.front: What does the radical 目 mean?
fields.extra: mù; an eye turned upright. In 看 (look: a hand shading an eye), 眼 (eye), 睡 (sleep). Hook vs the sun 日: the eye has TWO bars inside, like two eyelids.

fields.front: What does 月 mean as a character on its own?
fields.extra: yuè. A crescent moon. In 明 (bright: sun + moon together). But it leads a double life; see the next card.

fields.front: What does 月 usually mean on the LEFT side of a character?
fields.extra: There it is the compressed form of 肉 (ròu, meat), not the moon. Body parts everywhere: 脸 (face), 腿 (leg), 脑 (brain), 肚 (belly).

fields.front: What does the radical ⻊ (as in 跑) mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 足 (zú, foot). In 跑 (run), 跳 (jump), 踢 (kick): leg work.

fields.front: What does the radical 女 mean?
fields.extra: nǚ. In 妈 (mom), 姐 (older sister), 她 (she).

fields.front: What does the radical 子 mean?
fields.extra: zǐ; a swaddled baby with outstretched arms. In 孩 (child), 学 (study), 字 (written character).

fields.front: What does the radical 尸 mean?
fields.extra: shī; a slumped, seated body. It tops 尾 (tail), 屋 (room), and the deck's least dignified character, 尿 (urine).

fields.front: What does the radical 页 mean as a component?
fields.extra: yè; standalone it means 'page', but as a component it marks heads and faces: 顶 (top), 颜 (face), 题 (title).

fields.front: What does the radical 氵 (THREE drops) mean?
fields.extra: 'Three drops of water' (三点水), the side-form of 水 (shuǐ). In 河 (river), 海 (sea), 洗 (wash). Three drops = liquid; only two = ice.

fields.front: What does the radical 冫 (TWO drops) mean?
fields.extra: 'Two drops of water' (两点水). In 冰 (ice), 冷 (cold), 凉 (cool). Hook: ice is water with a drop frozen out; one drop short of 氵.

fields.front: What does the radical 火 mean?
fields.extra: huǒ; a little flame with sparks. In 烧 (burn), 灯 (lamp), 烤 (roast).

fields.front: What does the radical 灬 (four dots along the BOTTOM) mean?
fields.extra: Fire 火 flattened into four licks of flame under the pot: 热 (hot), 煮 (boil), 照 (shine).

fields.front: What does the radical 日 mean?
fields.extra: rì; a square sun with ONE bar inside (the eye 目 has two). Time and light: 早 (morning), 晚 (evening), 时 (time).

fields.front: What does the radical 木 mean?
fields.extra: mù; a trunk with branches and roots. In 树 (tree), 林 (woods: two trees), 桌 (table, made of wood).

fields.front: What does the radical 禾 mean?
fields.extra: hé; hook vs 木: a tree bowing under a drooping head of grain (the extra top stroke). In 种 (to plant), 秋 (autumn: grain + fire), 租 (rent, once paid in grain).

fields.front: What does the radical 艹 (across the TOP) mean?
fields.extra: 'Grass head' (草字头): two sprouts breaking soil. Caps 花 (flower), 茶 (tea), 药 (medicine, once herbal).

fields.front: What does the radical 土 mean?
fields.extra: tǔ; a mound on the ground. In 地 (ground), 城 (city), 坐 (sit: two people on the earth).

fields.front: What does the radical 山 mean?
fields.extra: shān; three peaks. In 岛 (island), 峰 (peak), 岸 (shore).

fields.front: What does the radical 石 mean?
fields.extra: shí; a cliff with a rock beneath it. In 破 (break), 硬 (hard), 碗 (bowl, once earthenware).

fields.front: What does the radical 田 mean?
fields.extra: tián; a bird's-eye view of paddy plots. In 男 (man: strength in the field) and 界 (boundary).

fields.front: What does the radical 雨 mean?
fields.extra: yǔ; drops falling from a cloud under the sky. Tops all weather: 雪 (snow), 雷 (thunder), 零 (zero, originally 'raindrop').

fields.front: What does the radical 宀 (the capped TOP) mean?
fields.extra: 'Treasure cover' (宝盖头). Everything domestic happens under it: 家 (home: a pig under the roof), 安 (peace), 室 (room).

fields.front: What does the radical 广 (a roof with an open left side) mean?
fields.extra: A lean-to open on one side, vs the fully enclosed roof 宀. Public and furniture-scale spaces: 店 (shop), 床 (bed), 座 (seat).

fields.front: What does the radical 门 mean?
fields.extra: mén; a doorframe. In 问 (ask: a mouth at the door), 间 (room), 闹 (noisy: a market at the gate).

fields.front: What does the radical 穴 mean?
fields.extra: xué; a roof over a dug-out opening. In 空 (empty), 穿 (to pierce through, hence 'to wear'), 窗 (window).

fields.front: What does the radical 辶 (the sled under a character) mean?
fields.extra: 'The walking radical' (走之底), a foot on a winding road. Carries 过 (pass), 送 (deliver), 远 (far): anything going somewhere.

fields.front: What does the radical 车 mean?
fields.extra: chē; a chariot seen from above, simplified. In 轮 (wheel), 转 (turn), 辆 (the measure word for cars).

fields.front: What does the radical 刂 (two vertical strokes on the RIGHT) mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 刀 (dāo, knife). In 刻 (carve), 别 (originally 'cut apart', now 'don't'), 划 (slash).

fields.front: What does the radical 力 mean?
fields.extra: lì; a flexed arm (or a plow). In 加 (add), 动 (move), 助 (help).

fields.front: What does the radical 又 mean?
fields.extra: yòu; a grasping hand in the oldest script. In 双 (pair: two hands), 友 (friend: hands joined), 对 (correct).

fields.front: What does the radical 攵 (on the RIGHT, like a crossed figure) mean?
fields.extra: A hand holding a stick. In 教 (teach), 放 (release), 改 (correct): discipline in every sense.

fields.front: What does the radical 讠 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 言 (yán, speech): words rising from a mouth. In 说 (speak), 话 (words), 请 (please). If a character has 讠, someone is talking.

fields.front: What does the radical 纟 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 糸, twisted silk strands. In 线 (thread), 纸 (paper), and 红 (red, from dyed silk): textiles and what came from them.

fields.front: What does the radical 钅 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 金 (jīn, gold/metal). In 钱 (money), 铁 (iron), 银 (silver): the whole periodic table's neighborhood.

fields.front: What does the radical 王 (on the LEFT of a character) mean?
fields.extra: It looks like 王 (king) but as a left-side radical it is 玉 (jade) missing its dot. Precious, playable things: 玩 (play), 球 (ball), 珍 (precious).

fields.front: What does the radical 贝 mean?
fields.extra: bèi; cowrie shells were ancient China's currency, so 贝 marks value: 贵 (expensive), 财 (wealth), 赚 (earn).

fields.front: What does the radical 衤 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 衣 (yī, clothes). In 裤 (pants), 袜 (socks), 被 (blanket). Hook vs 礻 (spirit): clothing has ONE MORE stitch (an extra dot).

fields.front: What does the radical 礻 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 示 (shì, an altar). In 神 (god), 祝 (bless), 福 (fortune). The plainer of the pair: spirits need no extra stitch, clothes 衤 do.

fields.front: What does the radical 饣 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 食 (shí, to eat). In 饭 (meal), 饿 (hungry), 饺 (dumpling). A menu's most important radical.

fields.front: What does the radical 米 mean?
fields.extra: mǐ; grains scattered around a cross. In 粉 (noodles/powder), 糖 (sugar), 粥 (congee).

fields.front: What does the radical 犭 mean?
fields.extra: The side-form of 犬 (quǎn, dog), extended to beasts in general: 狗 (dog), 猫 (cat), 猪 (pig).

fields.front: What does the radical 马 mean?
fields.extra: mǎ; mane and legs, simplified. In 骑 (ride) and 骂 (scold: two mouths yelling from horseback). Also the sound-lender in 妈 (mā, mom) and 吗 (ma, the question particle).

fields.front: What does the radical 牛 mean?
fields.extra: niú; a horned head from the front. In 物 (thing: oxen were THE possession) and 特 (special: an outstanding bull).

fields.front: What does the radical 鸟 mean?
fields.extra: niǎo; head, wing, and a claw. In 鸡 (chicken), 鸭 (duck), 鹅 (goose): your bird decks, in one stroke cluster.

fields.front: What does the radical 鱼 mean?
fields.extra: yú; head, scaled body, tail. In 鲜 (fresh: fish + lamb, the two things you want fresh) and 鲨 (shark).

fields.front: What does the radical 虫 mean?
fields.extra: chóng; generously defined: 蚊 (mosquito), but also 蛇 (snake) and 虾 (shrimp). Ancient taxonomy filed them all under 'bug'.

fields.front: What does the radical 疒 (a lean-to with two dots on its left) mean?
fields.extra: 'The sick radical' (病字旁): a person laid on a bed. Everything under it ails: 病 (ill), 疼 (ache), 瘦 (thin).

fields.front: What does the radical 阝 mean on the LEFT of a character?
fields.extra: From 阜 (fù, mound). In 阳 (the sunny side of the hill), 阴 (the shady side), 院 (courtyard). Mnemonic: hills on the left, towns on the right.

fields.front: What does the radical 阝 mean on the RIGHT of a character?
fields.extra: From 邑 (yì, settlement). In 都 (capital), 郊 (suburbs), 那 (that). Same glyph as the hill radical; only the side tells you which.
